$comment: Generative news-article corpus for the 2000s (digital live-timing / manufacturer-war online race-report voice), parsed by Companion.Core.News.NewsArticleBank. Crisp modern newswire cadence: stats-forward, sector times and telemetry to hand, global HD broadcast, manufacturer mega-budgets. Keys under 'bodies' are 'journalPhase|cause'; each holds era-keyed body templates selected deterministically. Bodies compose literal prose, fact tokens, and {pool:name} references into 'pools'. Fact tokens: {player} {team} {race} {year} {round} {position} {expected} {winner} {fieldSize} {champPosition} {champMove} {champLeader}. Add new eras/events by adding JSON only.

2000s: LIVE TIMING REPORT — {race}, {year}.
RACE CENTRE: {race}, round {round}.
FROM THE MEDIA CENTRE AT {race}.
ONLINE RACE REPORT — {race}, {year}.
PADDOCK WIRE — {race}, round {round}.
TIMING SCREENS LIVE — {race}, {year}.
PRESS ROOM FEED — {race}, round {round}.
TRACKSIDE DATA WIRE — {race}, {year}.
GLOBAL BROADCAST DESK — {race}, round {round}.
SECTOR-BY-SECTOR — {race}, {year}.
PIT WALL BULLETIN — {race}, round {round}.
The data goes back to the factory; the momentum stays with the team.

The timing sheets will read kindly in the Monday debrief.

Points banked, and a happy manufacturer board.
The telemetry says keep doing exactly that.
A result the marketing department will run with all week.
Green across the board on the debrief sheet.

Momentum, points, and a manufacturer board that's smiling.
Filed under 'do it again next round.'
default: A good day for the team.

2000s: The engineers will spend the flight home buried in the data.
A weekend the team will want scrubbed from the servers.
Questions for the debrief, and few easy answers.
The manufacturer board will want a better story next time.
Back to the simulator before the next round.
The data package flies home for a long night of analysis.
A weekend the strategists will replay frame by frame.
The manufacturer board wanted more, and the numbers know it.
Straight into the debrief, and no shortage of questions.
The simulator gets a heavy workload before the next round.
One for the 'lessons learned' folder on the server.
default: The team resets for the next round.
A day to file away and forget.
The team looks to the next round.

Standings update: {player} {champPosition}, {champMove}, {champLeader} still setting the benchmark.

The sector splits told the whole story.
Every timing loop had the {team} car in the green.

Purple sectors where it mattered most.

Fastest in two sectors, quickest through the speed trap.

2000s: The season's data is archived; the manufacturer program resets for next year.
The final standings are locked, and the offseason simulator work begins.
The championship servers go quiet until lights-out on a new campaign.
The telemetry archive is sealed and the winter development program spins up.
The standings are final, the servers idle, and the simulator work begins.
A season's worth of data goes to the factory; the new campaign is already on the whiteboard.
The championship feed signs off until the next lights-out.
The numbers are filed, the manufacturer resets, and the countdown to next year starts now.
default: The season is in the books.
The year is done; the next begins.

2000s: {pool:dateline} {player} takes the win at {race}, controlling the {year} race from the front for {team}. The gap over a {fieldSize}-car field never came under threat once the pit sequence played out. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} Victory logged: {player} wins {race} for {team}. Sector by sector the {team} car set the benchmark, and the broadcast graphics ran out of rivals to track. {pool:sectorNote} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} {player} beats a {expected} projection and then some — a flag-to-flag win at {race}. {team}'s manufacturer program has its result, and the live timing has a new fastest name. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} {player} converts front-running control into a {race} win for {team}. A {fieldSize}-car field never got a look-in once the {team} car cleared the opening sequence. {pool:sectorNote} {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

{pool:dateline} {player} off the pace at {race}, home {position} in a {fieldSize}-car field. The sector times flattered no one in the {team} garage this weekend. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}

{pool:dateline} Off-colour at {race}: {player} {position} for {team} when the form guide pointed higher. {winner} led a {fieldSize}-car field home while the {team} garage searched the screens. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}

2000s: {pool:dateline} {player} is out at {race} — a mechanical failure ends the run for {team}, no fault of the driver's. Reliability, not pace, wrote the result; {winner} took the win. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} Retirement at {race}: the {team} car let go while running its race and the telemetry flatlined. A {expected} projection counts for nothing once the machine stops. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} {player} parks the {team} car at {race} — a mechanical failure, no driver fault, and the telemetry cut out mid-run. {winner} took the win the reliability denied. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} Retirement at {race}: the {team} machine cried enough while {player} was in the fight. The data logs the failure; the standings log the zero. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} A DNF for {player} at {race}, the {team} car stopping with a problem no driver could out-drive. {expected} on paper counts for nothing once the engine note dies. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} Machinery, not the man: {player} is out of {race} with a mechanical failure for {team}. The timing screen went dark on a run that had promise. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} {player}'s {race} ends in the garage — a component lets go and the {team} car is done. {winner} led a {fieldSize}-car field to the flag. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} Reliability writes the result at {race}: {player} retires the {team} car, the telemetry flagging the failure before the driver could react. {pool:closeBad}
default: {player} retired from {race} with a mechanical failure. {pool:closeBad}

2000s: SEASON REVIEW — {year}. {player} closes the campaign as world champion, the {team} program topping the final standings after a title run the timing data flagged early. {pool:seasonClose}
The {year} title is settled and the number reads {champPosition}: {player} is champion, {team}'s manufacturer effort rewarded across a full season of points. {pool:seasonClose}

The standings are final and {player} sits {champPosition} — world champion for {team}, a season the telemetry called correctly all year. {pool:seasonClose}
